description: A "stub-tailed" wren: small wren with short tail, held cocked. The bird was first found while climbing up a tree trunk about 2 feet above ground, hopping from spot to spot, probing, as it moved upward. It was remarkably uniform rufescent/chestnut in color. Face looked uniform (I could not distinguish any streaking in auriculars) except a short, paler, poorly defined dash of supercilium starting behind eye. Back unbarred. Tiny tail was barred black on rufescent. Spotting/barring on underparts limited to lower belly. Golden buff checkering on folded primaries (that is, along lower edge of folded wing). Bill shorter than that of HOWR. Call typical djit-djit, somewhat resembling WIWA or RCKI heard frequently. See media
